────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  sevro   Helm chart cost & security analysis
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  source: demo
  5 workloads · 6 findings

   MED    api   save ~$29.20 /mo
    CPU request appears overprovisioned
    Request 2 vs limit 2.5 — typical utilization rarely justifies this
    ratio. Consider halving the request.
    confidence: ●●○ medium

   MED    api   save ~$2.79 /mo
    Memory request appears overprovisioned
    Request 1.6Gi vs limit 2Gi — typical utilization rarely justifies this
    ratio. Consider halving the request.
    confidence: ●●○ medium

   MED    cache
    Image not pinned to a stable tag
    Image redis:latest uses :latest. The same tag can resolve to different
    bytes across deploys; rollback becomes ambiguous. Pin to an immutable
    digest or version tag.
    confidence: ●●● high

   MED    logger
    Image not pinned to a stable tag
    Image fluentd uses (no tag). The same tag can resolve to different bytes
    across deploys; rollback becomes ambiguous. Pin to an immutable digest or
    version tag.
    confidence: ●●● high

   HIGH   worker
    Memory limit not set
    This workload declares a memory request but no limit. Without a limit it
    can consume node memory unbounded and evict neighbours under pressure. Set
    a limit at or above your observed P95.
    confidence: ●●● high

   LOW    worker
    CPU limit not set
    This workload declares a CPU request but no limit. Some teams omit CPU
    limits intentionally to avoid throttling; others expect predictable
    scheduling. Confirm this is the intended posture.
    confidence: ●●○ medium

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  estimated monthly savings: $31.99 /mo   (±40%)
  Sandbox accuracy: ±40%. Install the Sevro agent for exact numbers (sevro.dev/get).
  → install the agent for exact numbers: sevro.dev/get (https://sevro.dev/get)
